title: "screen res problem"

forum: General Help

### Post by Draaku on 2005-03-13
I upgraded and now I can not switch resolutions. If I switch to 1400x1050 the screen gets all garbled. It worked using warty, but after upgrading to hoary I can only use 1028x768. please could someone help me? I am using an IBM T41 laptop.

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"unix/:7100"			# local font server

### Post by sunscape on 2005-03-13
try to create a new xorg.conf file

as root (sudo): dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

Or, even better, install the fglrx drivers from synaptic and use fglrxconfig. 

Note: Your might have to edit the xorg.conf file afterwards to recognize your funky resolutions. There are several topics in the forum illustrating the solution.

---

### Post by Draaku on 2005-03-13
Hi, I'm not using xorg.conf. I am using XF86. I am total noob in Linux. I dont get it, it worked with previous version....help please.

---

### Post by Draaku on 2005-03-13
OK, I think I managed to fix, although I have no idea what I did...I also found out this new version uses x.org not XFree86. Learning as I go...thanks for the help.
